scenes.title: Meeting Someone
scenes.narrative: You step into a lively café in the Condesa neighborhood of Mexico City. The barista greets you warmly. Time to introduce yourself in Spanish.

scenes.title: Finding Your Way
scenes.narrative: You're standing outside the metro station in Mexico City's historic center. The Zócalo is nearby but the streets are a maze. Time to ask for directions.

words.sample_sentence: A la derecha está el museo.

scenes.title: Getting Food
scenes.narrative: The smell of grilled carne asada drifts from a taco stand. Your stomach rumbles. The taquero looks at you — ¿qué va a querer?

scenes.title: Shopping
scenes.narrative: You're browsing a colorful artisan market in Coyoacán. Beautiful handcrafted jewelry, pottery, and textiles surround you. Let's negotiate!

scenes.title: Emergency
scenes.narrative: Something's gone wrong — you're lost, not feeling well, or need help urgently. These are the words that could save your day.

scenes.title: At the Hotel
scenes.narrative: You arrive at your hotel in Roma Norte after a long flight. The receptionist greets you. Time to check in and find your room.

scenes.title: Small Talk
scenes.narrative: You're sharing a bench in Chapultepec Park with a friendly local. The conversation flows easily with these essential small talk words.

scenes.title: Time & Numbers
scenes.narrative: The taxi driver quotes a price, the museum closes at a certain hour, your flight is at a specific time. Numbers and time are everywhere.

scenes.title: Getting Around
scenes.narrative: Mexico City's metro is amazing but confusing. Taxis, Uber, and buses are everywhere. Here's how to get from point A to B.

scenes.title: Being Polite
scenes.narrative: Mexican culture is warm and courteous. These politeness words and phrases earn you instant goodwill and warmer interactions everywhere.

scenes.title: Basic Verbs
scenes.narrative: These core verbs are the engine of Spanish conversation. Master them and you can express almost any basic idea.

scenes.title: Pronouns & Connectors
scenes.narrative: The glue words that hold Spanish sentences together. Without them you'd sound choppy — with them, you can build flowing thoughts.

scenes.title: Common Adjectives
scenes.narrative: Describe the enormous pyramids, the tiny street dogs, the beautiful murals. These adjectives add color to everything you say.

scenes.title: Yes, No & Maybe
scenes.narrative: The simplest responses are often the most useful. Master these and you can handle any yes-or-no situation confidently.

scenes.title: People & Family
scenes.narrative: Family is at the heart of Mexican culture. Whether meeting relatives or talking about your own, these words come up constantly.

scenes.title: Food & Drink Essentials
scenes.narrative: Beyond the basics — you need to know your carne from your pollo, your cerveza from your jugo. This is Mexican culinary vocabulary.

scenes.title: Essential Questions
scenes.narrative: Questions are your superpower as a traveler. Each one opens a door — and shows locals you're genuinely making an effort.

scenes.title: Greetings & Farewells
scenes.narrative: Every Mexican encounter starts and ends warmly. Master these and you'll fit right in — from buenos días to hasta luego.

scenes.title: Feelings & Emotions
scenes.narrative: Your Mexican friend asks how you're really doing. Beyond 'bien,' here's how to express the full range of human emotion in Spanish.

scenes.title: Opinions & Thoughts
scenes.narrative: Share what you think — about the food, the city, the art. Mexicans love hearing visitors' genuine reactions to their country.

scenes.title: Making Plans
scenes.narrative: Your new friends want to go out tonight. Let's plan where, when, and what you'll do — in Spanish.

scenes.title: Describing Things
scenes.narrative: The pyramid is massive. The dog is tiny. The sky is incredible. Expand your vocabulary to paint the world with Spanish words.

scenes.title: At Work
scenes.narrative: Working remotely from a Mexican co-working space? Chatting with locals about their jobs? Here's your work vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: Mi oficina está en el centro.
Tengo una reunión a las dos.

scenes.title: Technology & Internet
scenes.narrative: Finding WiFi, ordering an Uber, sending a WhatsApp — modern life in Mexico requires these tech words.

scenes.title: Health & Body
scenes.narrative: Montezuma's revenge, altitude sickness, or just a headache. Know the body parts and health words to get help at a farmacia.

scenes.title: Nature & Weather
scenes.narrative: From Mexico City's parks to the coast's beaches, nature is everywhere. Here's how to talk about the world around you.

scenes.title: Entertainment
scenes.narrative: Mexico City's nightlife, concerts, museums, and lucha libre are legendary. Here's your entertainment vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: ¿Vamos al cine a ver una película?
Los niños juegan en el parque.

scenes.title: Relationships
scenes.narrative: From family bonds to new romances, Mexican social life revolves around close relationships. Here's the vocabulary of connection.

scenes.title: Daily Routine
scenes.narrative: From waking up to going to bed, describe the rhythm of your day. These words cover the daily basics.

scenes.title: Home & Living
scenes.narrative: Whether you're renting an apartment in CDMX or staying with a host family, these words help you talk about where you live.

words.sample_sentence: La comida está en la mesa.

scenes.title: Education & Learning
scenes.narrative: You're learning Spanish right now! Here are the words about studying, schools, and the joy of learning.

words.sample_sentence: El español es un idioma bonito.

scenes.title: Colors
scenes.narrative: From the bright pinks of Barragán architecture to the blue of the cenotes, Mexico is a explosion of color. Name what you see.

scenes.title: More Numbers
scenes.narrative: Beyond one, two, three — prices, addresses, and phone numbers require bigger numbers. Spanish numbers are logical after ten.

words.sample_sentence: Son las cuatro de la tarde.
A las seis de la mañana.

scenes.title: Clothing & Appearance
scenes.narrative: Shopping for huipiles? Describing what someone's wearing? From sombreros to huaraches, here's your fashion vocabulary.

scenes.title: Giving Directions
scenes.narrative: Now you can give directions too — and understand the detailed instructions locals give you with hand gestures and all.

scenes.title: Transportation
scenes.narrative: Combis, metro, taxis, and Uber — Mexico City has every mode of transport. Here's how to navigate them all.

words.sample_sentence: El autobús sale a las diez.
El avión sale a las tres.

scenes.title: Quantity & Size
scenes.narrative: More salsa? Less ice? A lot of cilantro? These words help you fine-tune exactly how much of something you want.

scenes.title: Animals
scenes.narrative: From street dogs to tropical birds to iguanas basking in the sun, Mexican wildlife is colorful and everywhere.

words.sample_sentence: El pez nada en el agua.

scenes.title: Days of the Week
scenes.narrative: Planning your itinerary? Markets are on certain days, museums close on Mondays. Know your lunes from your viernes.

words.sample_sentence: El domingo es día de familia.

scenes.title: Cooking & Kitchen
scenes.narrative: You join a cooking class and learn to make mole. The chef tosses chiles and spices — keep up with the instructions!

words.sample_sentence: La fruta del mercado es fresca.

scenes.title: Weather & Seasons
scenes.narrative: Mexico City's weather is famously mild, but rainy season can surprise you. Here's how to talk about it.

scenes.title: Money & Banking
scenes.narrative: Pesos, ATMs, tipping culture — navigate the financial side of Mexico with confidence.

scenes.title: Communication Verbs
scenes.narrative: Speaking, writing, reading, listening — the verbs of communication. Essential meta-language about language itself.

scenes.title: Religion & Culture
scenes.narrative: Mexico's culture is a vivid blend of indigenous and Spanish traditions. Understanding cultural vocabulary shows deep respect.

words.sample_sentence: El Día de los Muertos es especial.

scenes.title: More Useful Verbs
scenes.narrative: Expand your action vocabulary. These verbs come up constantly and will make you sound much more natural.

scenes.title: Time Expressions
scenes.narrative: Mexican time is flexible, but you still need to talk about when things happen — from 'ahorita' to 'la próxima semana'.

words.sample_sentence: El año pasado fui a Oaxaca.
A veces llueve por la tarde.

scenes.title: Shopping & Markets
scenes.narrative: From tianguis to supermarkets, Mexican shopping is an experience. Here's your expanded vocabulary for market adventures.

words.sample_sentence: La tienda cierra a las ocho.

scenes.title: Restaurant & Ordering
scenes.narrative: Beyond basic food words — how to navigate a Mexican menu, customize your order, and handle the bill like a local.

scenes.title: Verbs of Motion
scenes.narrative: Running, swimming, climbing — Mexico is an adventure. These action words keep you moving through it.

words.sample_sentence: Vamos a correr en el parque.
Vamos a nadar en el cenote.

scenes.title: Abstract Concepts
scenes.narrative: As your Spanish grows, express bigger ideas — time, life, truth, freedom. These abstract words elevate your conversations.

scenes.title: Expressing Needs
scenes.narrative: When you urgently need something — directions, medicine, a bathroom — these words get your point across immediately.

scenes.title: Places in Town
scenes.narrative: Navigating a new Mexican city? You need to know the bank, the hospital, the pharmacy, the park — here's your urban vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: El museo abre a las diez.

scenes.title: Comparisons & Superlatives
scenes.narrative: Which is bigger, better, cheaper? Spanish comparisons are elegant and logical — learn them and describe the world with precision.

scenes.title: Mexican Slang
scenes.narrative: Hanging out with locals in a cantina in Guadalajara, you'll hear colorful slang that no textbook teaches. These informal Mexican expressions make you sound like a true cuate.

words.sample_sentence: Ese mero es el que quiero.

scenes.title: Cooking Methods
scenes.narrative: You're taking a hands-on cooking class in Oaxaca. The chef demonstrates techniques as fragrant smoke rises from the comal. Time to learn the verbs of the kitchen.

words.sample_sentence: Voy a asar la carne en el carbón.
Hay que freír las tortillas en aceite.
Pica la cebolla en trozos pequeños.
Primero hay que pelar los chiles.

scenes.title: Geography
scenes.narrative: Mexico's landscape is stunningly diverse — from tropical jungles to desert valleys, volcanic mountains to Caribbean coasts. Describe the geography around you.

words.sample_sentence: Isla Mujeres es un lugar hermoso.
El río pasa por el pueblo.
El lago de Pátzcuaro es muy bonito.
El Valle de México es enorme.
La costa del Pacífico tiene playas increíbles.

scenes.title: Sports & Exercise
scenes.narrative: You join a pick-up soccer game at a local park, or hit the gym with your Mexican roommate. Sports vocabulary keeps you in the game.

words.sample_sentence: Mi deporte favorito es el fútbol.
Voy al gimnasio todos los días.
Entreno tres veces a la semana.

scenes.title: Music & Art
scenes.narrative: From mariachi in Plaza Garibaldi to murals by Rivera, Mexico pulses with artistic energy. Here's how to talk about the creative world.

words.sample_sentence: El Palacio de Bellas Artes tiene mucho arte.
Esa pintura es de Frida Kahlo.

scenes.title: Social Media
scenes.narrative: Your new Mexican friends want to follow you online. Social media is universal, but the Spanish vocabulary for it has its own flavor.

words.sample_sentence: Te voy a seguir en redes sociales.
Dejé un comentario en tu foto.

scenes.title: Business
scenes.narrative: You're meeting a potential business partner at a trendy café in Polanco. From pitching ideas to closing deals, these words keep you professional.

words.sample_sentence: El cliente siempre tiene la razón.
Nuestro producto es de alta calidad.
La ganancia fue buena este trimestre.

scenes.title: Legal & Official
scenes.narrative: Renewing a visa, signing a rental agreement, or filling out forms at a government office — bureaucracy requires its own vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: El registro civil está en el centro.

scenes.title: Advanced Emotions
scenes.narrative: Beyond happy and sad — express the nuanced feelings of daily life. Frustrated by traffic? Proud of your Spanish progress? Say it with precision.

words.sample_sentence: Me siento ansioso antes del examen.

scenes.title: Household Items
scenes.narrative: Settling into your Airbnb or staying with a host family, you need to know the stuff that fills a Mexican home — from blankets to fans.

words.sample_sentence: La ropa está en el armario.
Hay un espejo en el baño.

scenes.title: Garden & Plants
scenes.narrative: Mexican gardens burst with bougainvillea, cactus, and tropical flowers. Whether tending a rooftop garden or visiting a botanical park, here's your green vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: Las flores de cempasúchil son naranjas.
Mi abuela siembra tomates en el jardín.
Las plantas crecen rápido con la lluvia.

scenes.title: Parties & Celebrations
scenes.narrative: Mexican celebrations are legendary — from quinceañeras to posadas, Día de los Muertos to casual weekend fiestas. Join the party with the right words.

words.sample_sentence: Recibí una invitación a la boda.
Vamos a celebrar con una cena.
La decoración de la fiesta está preciosa.
El pastel de tres leches está delicioso.

scenes.title: Marine Life
scenes.narrative: Snorkeling in the cenotes of the Yucatán or diving off Cozumel, Mexico's waters teem with life. Here's your underwater vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: El arrecife de coral es espectacular.
Vimos una tortuga marina en la playa.
Los delfines nadan cerca del barco.
Las olas están muy grandes hoy.
Rentamos equipo de snorkel en la playa.

scenes.title: Mexican Drinks
scenes.narrative: Beyond beer and tequila, Mexico has a world of traditional drinks — from sweet horchata to tangy tamarindo. Refresh yourself with these words.

words.sample_sentence: Una horchata bien fría, por favor.

words.meaning: beer with lime, salt, and chili

words.sample_sentence: El pulque es una bebida prehispánica.
El agua de jamaica es mi favorita.
Un agua de tamarindo con chile.

scenes.title: Crafts & Artisanry
scenes.narrative: Mexico's artisan traditions stretch back millennia. From Oaxacan pottery to Chiapas textiles, these words connect you to the creative heritage.

words.sample_sentence: Este plato es de barro negro.
La cerámica de Talavera es famosa.
Los tejidos de Chiapas son coloridos.
Taxco es famoso por su plata.
La blusa tiene un bordado precioso.

scenes.title: Daily Errands
scenes.narrative: Running everyday errands in Mexico — waiting in line at the bank, sending a package, doing laundry. These practical words save you time.

words.sample_sentence: La fila en el banco es larga.

scenes.title: Mexican Cuisine
scenes.narrative: Beyond tacos — Mexico's cuisine is a UNESCO treasure. From complex mole sauces to steaming tamales, these are the dishes every visitor must know.

words.sample_sentence: El mole poblano lleva más de veinte ingredientes.
Los tamales de mi abuela son los mejores.
El pozole rojo es mi favorito.
Quiero un elote con mayonesa y chile.
Vamos por unos churros con chocolate.
El guacamole tiene aguacate, limón y cilantro.

scenes.title: Environmental
scenes.narrative: Mexico faces real environmental challenges — and its people are increasingly eco-conscious. Talk about sustainability and the natural world.

words.sample_sentence: No tires basura en la calle.
La contaminación en la ciudad es un problema.
Me encanta estar en la naturaleza.
Hay que cuidar el medio ambiente.

scenes.title: Describing People
scenes.narrative: Talking about someone's character is essential in social settings. Whether praising a new friend or warning about a sketchy taxi driver, these words help.

words.sample_sentence: La gente aquí es muy amable.
No seas tacaño, invita la cena.

scenes.title: Travel Logistics
scenes.narrative: Booking confirmations, schedule changes, cancellations — the behind-the-scenes vocabulary of travel that keeps your trip running smoothly.

words.sample_sentence: Hice una reserva para dos personas.
El producto tiene un año de garantía.

scenes.title: Clothing Details
scenes.narrative: At a tailor's shop in San Miguel de Allende, or browsing traditional huipiles, you need the vocabulary to describe fabrics and garment details.

words.sample_sentence: Quiero una camisa de manga corta.
Guarda las llaves en el bolsillo.
Esta tela es de buena calidad.
El rebozo de seda es hermoso.
El suéter de lana es calientito.

scenes.title: Kitchen Utensils
scenes.narrative: Cooking in your Airbnb kitchen or helping your host family prepare dinner, you'll need to ask for the right tools.

words.sample_sentence: Pon el agua a hervir en la olla.
Calienta el aceite en la sartén.
Pon los ingredientes en la licuadora.

scenes.title: Professions
scenes.narrative: Meeting people from all walks of life — from lawyers to journalists, engineers to nurses. Know how to ask and describe what people do for a living.

words.sample_sentence: La enfermera me tomó la presión.
La periodista escribió un buen artículo.

scenes.title: Body Actions
scenes.narrative: At the pharmacy explaining symptoms, or just describing what happens when you catch a cold at 2,200 meters altitude — your body has its own vocabulary.

scenes.title: Textures & Materials
scenes.narrative: Touch the rough volcanic stone, the smooth obsidian, the soft alpaca wool. Mexico is a sensory experience — describe what you feel.

situations.title: At the Airport
situations.narrative: You've landed at Mexico City's Benito Juárez International Airport. The altitude hits you as you exit the plane. Navigate customs and find your ride.

words.sample_sentence: La fila de migración es larga.
El vuelo tiene un retraso de dos horas.

situations.title: Getting Around CDMX
situations.narrative: Mexico City's transport system is vast — the metro is cheap and efficient, combis are an adventure, and Uber is everywhere. Navigate like a local.

words.sample_sentence: En la esquina de la calle.
Necesitas hacer transbordo en Pino Suárez.
Compra una tarjeta de metro en la taquilla.
Hay ciclovía en Paseo de la Reforma.
Conozco un atajo para evitar el tráfico.

situations.title: Mexican Food & Restaurants
situations.narrative: From tacos al pastor at midnight to chilaquiles for breakfast, Mexico City's food scene is unparalleled. Order like a local.

words.sample_sentence: Tres tacos al pastor, por favor.
Un mezcal con sal de gusano.
La comida corrida incluye sopa y postre.
La fonda de la esquina tiene comida casera.

situations.title: At the Hotel / Airbnb
situations.narrative: You're staying in a charming colonia in Roma Norte. Here's how to handle check-in, requests, and daily needs.

words.sample_sentence: La regadera no tiene agua caliente.
Mi habitación está en el tercer piso.
Guarda tu pasaporte en la caja fuerte.

situations.title: Shopping & Markets
situations.narrative: From the massive Mercado de la Merced to boutiques in Polanco, shopping in CDMX is an adventure. Bargain, browse, and buy.

words.sample_sentence: Hay una oferta de dos por uno.
El tianguis de los martes tiene de todo.

situations.title: Sightseeing & Culture
situations.narrative: Teotihuacán pyramids, Frida Kahlo's house, Xochimilco canals — Mexico City is a cultural wonderland. Here's how to explore it.

words.sample_sentence: La pirámide del Sol es enorme.
Hay una exposición temporal de Frida Kahlo.
Desde el mirador se ve toda la ciudad.
Visitamos una galería de arte contemporáneo.

situations.title: Emergency in Mexico
situations.narrative: You hope you won't need these, but just in case — from stomach bugs to lost wallets, be prepared for the unexpected.

words.sample_sentence: ¿Tiene pastillas para el dolor de cabeza?
